story.title: The Night of the Storm Concert
story.body: Professor Alam owned a small music school situated along the main road. Every year, his band of students performed on stage at the town festival. This year, the schedule was set — the concert was to begin within two hours. The students had barely finished rehearsing when dark clouds appeared in the sky. A heavy storm was predicted to hit the area. The professor sent a message to the organizers suggesting they cancel the event. But the band refused. They noticed the garbage had already been cleared from the driveway and the stage was ready. A student named Mira said, 'This is our own moment. We cannot let a storm take it away.' The professor smiled. He made a quick list of safety steps and shared it with the team. They rushed to cover the instruments and set up shelter. Within an hour, they were ready. When the storm passed, the band walked onto the stage and played with pure magic. The audience, who had barely left their seats, erupted in applause. It was no longer a boring night — it was the most unforgettable concert the town had ever seen. The professor stood at the side of the stage, proud that his students had predicted something he had not — that courage is the greatest instrument of all.

comprehension.q: What did the professor first suggest?
What did Mira say about the concert?
opts: it was their own moment they could not lose

comprehension.q: What was the greatest instrument according to the story?

listening.title: Planning the School Concert
listening.script:
A: Did you get my message about the concert schedule?
B: Yes! But I barely had time to read it.
A: The professor wants us to share the list of instruments needed.
B: I noticed some are missing from the stage.
A: Should we cancel the rehearsal?
B: No. Rush over and let us fix it within the hour.
A: A storm is predicted for tonight.
B: I know. The professor owns a backup plan.
A: Do you think the band will still perform?
B: Of course. This is our own stage. We cannot let garbage or storms stop us.
A: What if the audience finds it boring?
B: With this band? Pure magic. Trust me.
A: Fine. I will send a message to the driveway team.
B: Good. Follow the schedule and everything will be fine.

comprehension.q: What does B say about the band's performance?

speaking.prompt: Describe an exciting event you attended. Use words like stage, band, magic, notice, and schedule.
speaking.example: I once attended a concert where a local band performed on stage. I noticed the schedule had changed, but it did not matter. The music was pure magic. Within minutes, everyone forgot how the evening had barely started as boring and became fully alive.
tips: Describe where the event was held
Mention what you noticed about the stage or band
End with how the magic of the event made you feel
